data.content:
Now I have a complete picture. Here are all the **legacy patterns** I found:

| # | Legacy Pattern | SDK-style Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="...">` | Replace with `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` |
| 2 | Manual `Import` of `Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` | Implicit via SDK |
| 3 | Explicit `Configuration`/`Platform` defaults | SDK provides these |
| 4 | `ProjectGuid` | Not needed |
| 5 | `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | SDK defaults |
| 6 | `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | → `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| 7 | Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks | SDK defaults match these exactly |
| 8 | Explicit `<Reference>` for framework assemblies | Implicit in SDK-style |
| 9 | Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | SDK auto-globs all `.cs` files |
| 10 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Attributes auto-generated; metadata moves into csproj |

Let me apply the modernization now.

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the legacy project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and modernize the project file to SDK-style format.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **Old-style `<Project>` element** with `ToolsVersion` and `xmlns` — SDK-style uses `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
2. **Manual `Import` of `Microsoft.Common.props` and `Microsoft.CSharp.targets`** — handled automatically by SDK
3. **Explicit `Configuration` and `Platform` properties** — SDK provides sensible defaults
4. **`ProjectGuid`** — not needed in SDK-style


5. **`FileAlignment`**, **`AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`**, and **`Deterministic`** — all defaults in SDK-style
6. **Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks** — SDK handles these with sensible defaults
7. **Explicit framework assembly references** like `System` and `System.Core` — implicitly included in SDK-style
8. **Explicit `<Compile>` items** — SDK-style uses globbing to automatically include all `.cs` files
9. **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — SDK-style generates this automatically from csproj properties
10. **`TargetFrameworkVersion` v4.7.2** — converts to `TargetFramework: net472`

Now I'm creating the modernized csproj with the SDK format, setting the target framework and assembly metadata...

Writing modernized csproj...

Since the project file is named `LegacyApp.csproj`, the `RootNamespace` and `AssemblyName` both default to `LegacyApp`, so I can safely omit them for a cleaner file. I should also remove the `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` file since the csproj now handles all that metadata directly.

</Project>

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

data.content:
Done. The project went from **47 lines → 11 lines**. Here's a summary of what changed:

**Removed (all handled by SDK defaults):**
- `ToolsVersion`, `xmlns`, and both `Import` statements
- `ProjectGuid`, `Configuration`, `Platform`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`
- `OutputType` (defaults to `Library`)
- `RootNamespace`/`AssemblyName` (default to project filename `LegacyApp`)
- Both Debug/Release property groups (SDK defaults match exactly)
- All 6 framework `<Reference>` items (implicit in SDK-style)
- All `<Compile>` items (SDK auto-globs `**/*.cs`)
- `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` (metadata moved into csproj properties; SDK auto-generates assembly attributes)

**Kept as csproj properties:** `Description`, `Company`, `Copyright`, `Version` — migrated from `AssemblyInfo.cs`.
